definition: Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) automates the process of integrating, testing, and delivering machine learning models into production environments. It ensures consistent and repeatable workflows for ML code, data, and infrastructure.
use_cases: Automating data and model validation in pipelines
Continuous retraining and redeployment upon new data availability
Integrating infrastructure provisioning with deployment

definition: Data Engineering in MLOps focuses on creating scalable, reliable, and observable data pipelines for machine learning workflows. It involves orchestrating ETL tasks, maintaining data quality, and ensuring availability for downstream training jobs.
use_cases: Orchestrating ETL and feature extraction pipelines using Dagster
Ensuring data quality with validation and schema enforcement
Managing dependencies and scheduling across distributed systems

definition: Machine Learning Engineering bridges the gap between research and production. It includes experiment tracking, model reproducibility, packaging, and lifecycle management of ML models in collaboration with orchestration systems.
use_cases: Tracking experiments and parameters using MLflow
Registering and versioning models in MLflow Model Registry
Integrating with Dagster to orchestrate training, evaluation, and deployment stages
Packaging models for reproducible serving environments

definition: Model Deployment in MLOps involves automating the process of packaging, provisioning, and serving machine learning models to production environments. Infrastructure-as-Code tools like OpenTofu enable reproducible and maintainable deployment workflows.
use_cases: Defining infrastructure (Cloud Run, Artifact Registry) using OpenTofu configuration
Automating containerized model deployments via CI/CD pipelines
Managing secure deployments to GCP Cloud Run from a centralized model registry
Integrating MLflow model artifacts with Google Artifact Registry for versioned deployments

definition: Model Monitoring ensures the performance, fairness, and reliability of ML systems post-deployment. It identifies data drift, model decay, and operational issues to trigger retraining or rollback mechanisms.
use_cases: Tracking model accuracy and latency in production
Detecting input data drift or concept drift
Triggering automated retraining pipelines using monitored signals

definition: A Feature Store serves as a centralized system for storing, managing, and serving machine learning features consistently across training and inference environments.
use_cases: Maintaining offline and online feature consistency
Providing discoverability and reusability of features
Versioning and lineage tracking of feature sets

definition: Model Versioning manages the lifecycle of machine learning models by tracking their versions, configurations, and deployment history for reproducibility and compliance.
use_cases: Comparing performance metrics across model versions
Tracing data, code, and environment dependencies for each version
Rolling back to previous stable model versions upon failure
